scope_note: QA Three-Phase Cosmos Cancellation cert [303]. Primary sources: Hardy, G.H. and Wright, E.M. (2008) An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers, Oxford, ISBN 978-0-19-921986-5, Ch.X (Fibonacci matrix); Wall, D.D. (1960) Fibonacci Primitive Roots, Amer. Math. Monthly 67(6):525-532 DOI 10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Pisano periods); Hestenes, D. and Sobczyk, G. (1984) Clifford Algebra to Geometric Calculus, Reidel, ISBN 978-90-277-1673-6 (versor grading). CLAIM: (C1) M^0+M^8+M^16 equiv 3I mod 9: the three matrices at 0°/120°/240° on the mod-24 clock sum to 3 times the identity, not zero. (C2) det(M^8)=+1: the 8-step inter-phase advance is even-grade (rotor); the 1-step T advance is odd-grade (versor); Cassini identity det(M^k)=(-1)^k verified k=0..24. (C3) M^8 has order exactly 3 mod 9: M^8 not I, M^16 not I, M^24=I; M^8 equiv [[4,3],[3,7]], M^16 equiv [[7,6],[6,4]] mod 9. (C4) T^8 maps all 72 Cosmos states (v3(gcd(b,e))=0) to Cosmos; the 72 states partition into exactly 24 disjoint triads {(b,e), T^8(b,e), T^16(b,e)} with T^24(b,e)=(b,e) for all Cosmos (b,e); exhaustive A1 no-zero arithmetic. (C5) Observer layer (Theorem NT): exact rational sum 1+(-1/2)+(-1/2)=0 confirms cos(0°)+cos(120°)+cos(240°)=0; the continuous balance is an observer-layer fact only — the discrete QA sum is 3I not 0, confirming the Theorem NT boundary. Builds on cert [291] (pi(9)=24, M^24=I mod 9), cert [296] (T=M det=-1 odd-grade versor, M^2=LR rotor), cert [298] (1+8+72 v3-stratification), cert [299] (Cassini det(M^k)=(-1)^k). Lineage: derived 2026-06-02.
